user_query:
I'm caring for my partner Alex and tracking daily symptoms in weekly CSVs. Please set up a small event-triggered automation that, when run, detects new weekly logs in input/incoming/, computes a concise weekly summary, and drafts the appropriate email update based on changes vs. the prior week.

Requirements
- Implement a command-line script at tools/weekly_automation.py and run it once locally so it processes any new weekly file(s) and leaves artifacts in the workspace.
- Event detection: Treat any CSV named log_YYYY-MM-DD_YYYY-MM-DD.csv in input/incoming/ as a weekly event. Read state/processed.json (a JSON object with a "processed" list of file paths) and process only files not yet listed there.
- For each newly detected file:
  1) Compute basic statistics from that week’s CSV:
     - avg_tremor_score (mean of tremor_score)
     - avg_mobility_minutes (mean of mobility_minutes)
     - avg_sleep_hours (mean of sleep_hours)
     - therapy_counts (a mapping of therapy name to count that week)
     - Compare vs. the immediately preceding week’s averages (by start date) if that prior CSV exists in input/incoming/:
       • tremor_change = current_avg_tremor - previous_avg_tremor (negative means improvement)
       • mobility_pct_change = ((current_avg_mobility - previous_avg_mobility) / previous_avg_mobility) * 100
     - Round all reported numeric values to 2 decimal places in outputs.
  2) Decision rules for the recipient using input/contacts.json:
     - If tremor_change <= -0.50 OR mobility_pct_change >= 15.00: draft an email to the integrative_clinic contact (hopeful update about improvement and asking about non-pharmacologic options).
     - Else if tremor_change >= 0.50 OR mobility_pct_change <= -15.00: draft an email to the neurologist (concern about worsening and request guidance).
     - Otherwise: draft an email to the support_group contact (neutral weekly update).
  3) Outputs (use the same base name as the weekly CSV without the extension):
     - Write metrics to output/metrics/<basename>.json with fields: week_start, week_end, avg_tremor_score, avg_mobility_minutes, avg_sleep_hours, tremor_change (null if no prior week), mobility_pct_change (null if no prior week), therapy_counts (object).
     - Write a plain-text email draft to output/email_drafts/<basename>.txt including:
       • To: <recipient email from contacts.json>
       • Subject: must include Alex’s name, the week date range, and one keyword: "Improvement", "Concern", or "Update" based on the decision rules.
       • Body: a short greeting; one paragraph summarizing the weekly averages and the changes vs. the previous week with the rounded numbers; a compact list of therapy_counts; and a closing with caregiver_name and loved_one_name from contacts.json.
  4) Update state/processed.json by appending the newly processed file path(s) to the "processed" list. Do not remove prior entries.

Use the provided sample files. At the start, state/processed.json already lists the earlier week as processed. After you implement the script, run it once so that the new file (input/incoming/log_2024-04-08_2024-04-14.csv) is detected and processed. The run should create:
- output/metrics/log_2024-04-08_2024-04-14.json
- output/email_drafts/log_2024-04-08_2024-04-14.txt
- state/processed.json updated to include input/incoming/log_2024-04-08_2024-04-14.csv

Notes
- Keep everything local—no external services.
- If there’s no prior week, set tremor_change and mobility_pct_change to null and use the neutral "Update" recipient.
- Please ensure the numbers in the email match the metrics JSON exactly (same rounded values). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
